name: Oat & Onion Tarts
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the oats, flours, cheddar cheese, and a pinch each of salt, paprika, and cayenne.
2. Cut in then rub in the fat until combined with the dry ingredients and water.
3. Rest the dough.
4. Peel, quarter, and slice onions wafer thin. Toss them in melted butter and let them sweat over very gentle heat for about 20 minutes or until meltingly tender, stirring occasionally.
5. Roll out half of the pastry in two batches, keeping the other half covered. Use it to line mini mincepie tins or individual 4-inch fluted flan tins. Patch any tears with your fingertips.
6. Blind bake on a pre-heated baking sheet at 400°F (200°C) gas mark 6 for 12 minutes with greaseproof paper and beans, then another 12 minutes without the lining.
7. When onions are soft, remove the lid and cook over increased heat until golden brown. Season generously with salt and black pepper, add a dash of cayenne and paprika, and mix well.
8. Drain onions in a sieve over a bowl for a few minutes to cool them slightly. Beat eggs with soured cream, add onions, and check seasoning.
9. Spoon the mixture into tarts, sprinkle grated cheese on top, and bake at 400°F (200°C) gas mark 6 for 20-25 minutes until puffed up and golden.
